SAP Future of PMM Training
Chapter 8 Learning Guide
futureofpmm.com

From Signed to Advocate

Key concepts and takeaways from Chapter 8

Learning Objectives

  • Understand how customers use AI to evaluate renewal decisions
  • Build always-on value surfacing that's proactive, not campaign-driven
  • Detect expansion signals through AI-powered usage analysis
  • Create advocacy content that AI agents discover and cite

The Renewal Risk

Before talking to CSMs, customers ask AI: "Should I renew?" AI provides:

  • Competitive alternatives they may not have known
  • Pricing benchmarks for their contract
  • Feature gaps vs. competitors
  • Any negative news or concerns about you
Key Insight: Renewals are now contested by default. Every customer has an AI advisor suggesting they "evaluate alternatives" — even happy customers.

Always-On Value Surfacing

Content TypePurpose
Usage DashboardsReal-time metrics: time saved, costs avoided, outcomes achieved
Benchmark Reports"You're in the top 20% of implementations" — quotable proof
ROI TrackingDocumented return with specific numbers, updated regularly
Roadmap TransparencyAI can say "this gap will be addressed in Q3"

Expansion Signal Detection

SignalWhat It Indicates
Usage TrajectoryApproaching limits → tier upgrade opportunity
Feature ExplorationTrying premium features → cross-sell opportunity
User GrowthNew team onboarding → license expansion
Support PatternsAdvanced questions → training/services opportunity

Traditional vs. AI-Era Case Studies

TraditionalAI-Era
PDF downloads behind formsPublic HTML pages, indexed and quotable
Vague outcomes ("improved efficiency")Specific metrics ("32% cost reduction")
Stock photos and marketing speakNamed customers, verifiable claims
Static, never updatedUpdated with new outcomes

Creating AI-Discoverable Advocacy

  • Public customer pages — Ungated with specific outcomes, quotable metrics
  • Video testimonials with transcripts — Searchable, indexable, citable
  • Customer community content — Forums and discussions AI can surface
  • Review presence — G2, Gartner Peer Insights, TrustRadius

Structuring Advocacy for AI

  • Schema.org markup — Review, Organization, Product schemas
  • Quotable headlines — Lead with the metric: "Acme reduced processing time by 47%"
  • Industry tagging — Clear categorization for AI matching
  • Outcome taxonomy — Consistent language (cost reduction, time savings, etc.)

Key Takeaways

  1. Customers query AI about renewals before talking to CSMs — be prepared for contested renewals
  2. Value surfacing must be continuous, not campaign-driven — AI can be queried any day
  3. Usage patterns create expansion signals that AI can detect and help action
  4. Advocacy content must be public, specific, and AI-discoverable to influence prospects
  5. Case studies behind forms are invisible to AI — make outcomes quotable and indexed
